programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries
programme.desc: Examining strange happenings, including a news producer, who finds out the truth behind his adoption, and a popular theatre student, who goes missing without a trace

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey's partying catches up with him when he wakes up in a strange bed and ends up being grilled at Mill Street station on rape charges. Will takes a blood sample from an unconscious driver who has knocked a courier off his bike, and takes advice from Matt about whether it is admissible evidence in court. When the sample goes missing, he jumps to conclusions about his love-rival's determination to make life difficult for him

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Honey is horrified to learn Matt has taken over all the wedding arrangements and warns Emma not to rush blindly into marriage. A prostitute found beaten unconscious turns out to be a drugs mule working for Paul Haskew and Jack finally realises how deeply Zain is involved in the criminal activities of Kristen Shaw. Sam angles for a date with Phil and Stuart is told to mind his own business

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey investigates the disappearance of a prostitute whose evidence helped put away rapist Martin Delaney. Zain finally persuades Kristen Shaw to name her supplier and puts himself in great danger to arrange a meeting with the mysterious Jose Alvarez. Phil apologises to Sam for missing their date and agrees to take things slowly, but Stuart makes it clear he's not going to be pushed out without a fight

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A young tearaway is brought in for questioning about a break-in at a doctor's surgery, but it soon becomes clear a staff member has something to hide. Yvonne loses confidence about her chances of landing the sergeant's job at Sun Hill and decides to look elsewhere for promotion. Emma chooses the wrong moment to tell Matt about her night of passion with Will

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Miss Matty confides in Mary about Mr Holbrook's death, prompting her to inform Major Gordon of Jessie's regret over not marrying him. Dr Harrison tells Rev Hutton of his intentions toward Sophy and asks permission to court her, blissfully unaware that the generosity he has shown to Mrs Rose has accidentally created an unlikely love triangle. Penultimate episode of the costume drama, starring Judi Dench and Simon Woods

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The true nature of friendship within Cranford is revealed as the ladies rally to help Miss Matty when she receives terrible news. Meanwhile, Dr Harrison suffers as his career, reputation and relationship all seem ruined, while Miss Galindo and Mr Carter grow closer as they face up to the tragedy of Hanbury. Judi Dench, Philip Glenister and Simon Woods star

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: First in a two-part special. The story picks up in August 1844, two years after Miss Matty lost her beloved sister Deborah. Mr Buxton has returned to live quietly in Cranford following the death of his wife, although Miss Matty is concerned about his relationship with his son as neither seem to be coping with their bereavement. The local busybody is also worried about Peggy Bell, who lives in an isolated cottage looking after her inattentive mother and domineering brother, so she hatches a plot to bring these young residents together. However, the surprise arrival of Lady Ludlow's son Lord Septimus leads to a chain of unexpected events concerning the looming railway, and a tragedy at Matty's own house forces her to re-evaluate her opinions on the subject. Judi Dench and Jonathan Pryce star

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Fifteen Streets
programme.desc: Feature-length drama based on Catherine Cookson's novel set in early 20th-century Newcastle. Worker John O'Brien lives near poverty in a small house in the workers district, yet falls in love with teacher Mary, daughter of a rich family. Despite the pressures exerted by the social difference, the vigorous Mary refuses to allow outer circumstances destroy their relationship. Starring Sean Bean and Clare Holman

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Tony worries Bren will be distracted during her TV debut. Stan hopes for a proposal and Dolly is so upset at the design of the new uniforms that she considers drastic action. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Thelma Barlow

programme.title: dinnerladies diaries
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the sitcom, featuring interviews with the cast as they reminisce about the show on its 20th anniversary

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty and amiable sidekick Geoffrey become embroiled in a convoluted case involving a deaf birdwatcher who goes missing at the same time as a woman's body is found at the bottom of a cliff he is known to frequent. Light-hearted drama, with Patricia Routledge and Dominic Monaghan, featuring a guest appearance by David Bower

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nurses and midwives of Nonnatus House prepare to celebrate Sister Monica Joan's birthday, but plans are put on hold when the closure of a nearby maternity home leaves them inundated with new patients, many of whom are in urgent need of support. Lucille treats a woman whose father is offering shelter to an old friend suffering with memory loss, and as November 22, 1963 arrives, the residents of Poplar are rocked by shocking news from America

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: In the tense build-up to a world trade summit in Edinburgh, Rebus looks forward to a well-earned day off - until Siobhan rings to say a body has been found in the grounds of the hotel hosting the conference. When a government minister is later killed in a fall from the castle ramparts, the abrasive copper comes up against Commander Steelforth of the SO12 unit providing security for the summit and, not for the first time, finds himself at odds with the Establishment. Julie Graham guest stars, with Ken Stott and Claire Price

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Fifteen-year-old Cassie Booth goes missing while doing her local paper round, and her blood-stained clothes are later found in a nearby boathouse owned by Karl Wilding. He is the prime suspect, but then seemingly helpful Stephen Warrington, a wine merchant who has initially brought a complaint about a house being used as a brothel, demonstrates too much interest in the case

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A doctor is accused of murdering a seriously ill patient with a morphine overdose, a case which echoes a dilemma in Deed's personal life and sets him on the path to potential ruin. Meanwhile, Charlie's HIV-positive friend finds herself in danger of losing her baby to Social Services, and calls on the judge for help. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester and William are disappointed to learn Emma and Peter have made arrangements to be elsewhere over the festive period, so decide to treat their new French friends and neighbours to a typically English Christmas. Seasonal special from 1990, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.desc: Ian is trying to find some dirt on Phil, Arthur is campaigning to stop an incinerator from being built and Sharon discloses her master plan to Michelle

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Medical drama about a practice set in the Derbyshire countryside. The doctors wrestle with the concept of assisted death.

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried deals with a miserly scrap merchant notorious for mistreating his horses and comes up trumps. Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy star

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: William and Hester tell daughter Emma about their decision to move to France, and set about finding somewhere suitable to live. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana expose a fake spiritualist, and the Bayview males demonstrate their karate-chopping prowess for Jane. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Von Klinkerhoffen orders Rene and Edith to dress up as Hitler and Goering pretending to leave town so he can catch any Resistance group that tries to attack them. Meanwhile, Michelle plans to swap an eel containing the Fallen Madonna for a dog fish containing 10 million French francs

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly suggests Clegg perform a ritual exorcism on Howard's bicycle when it starts acting like a machine possessed in the presence of Pearl. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Part two of two. The amateur sleuths continue their investigation after Martin Frazer's cousin, who supposedly drowned, is spotted alive and well a few days later. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The leader of a spiritual retreat is found strangled en route to the contemplation garden, giving Jack plenty to think about as he tries to figure out which member of the group was responsible. The problem is, they were all meditating at the time of the murder, and are able to provide one another with alibis, so how could any of them be the killer? To add to the DI's problems, he is struggling to come up with a speech to honour Commissioner Patterson at an award ceremony. Caribbean whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and the team investigate why five years earlier a man seemingly vanished during his morning run and has not been seen since. The alarm was raised when he never made it to work at the construction company belonging to his father-in-law, who has a history of violence, and the detectives' suspicions grow when they realise how much the missing man's wife is scared of her dad. Could he have had something to do with his son-in-law's disappearance? Amanda Redman, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Denis Lawson and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The agents are assigned to protect a journalist about to break the story of a former Cabinet minister with a shady past who was murdered by a neo-conservative group - which happens to have connections with the government. Douglas Hodge guest stars, with Rupert Penry-Jones and Olga Sosnovska

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Dan takes a shine to his flirtatious 'girlfriend' when he goes undercover in a sting operation to entrap a fraudulent currency dealer - and turns up late for his first serious outing in the boxing ring. Stuart and Sam investigate an arson attack on the home of a foster carer which seems to be the work of a disgruntled former charge. June finally hears the news she's been waiting for from Rod

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: As Pinkerton looks at the Greens more closely, they join forces to hide an ugly secret. McBurney, hoping to distance Foster from Mary, sends him to a nearby army camp on a house call. Alice helps Frank escape and Samuel earns Charlotte's respect

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Drama set in 1895, chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. The first episode sees Eleanor embarking on her medical career _ and immediately crossing swords with eminent surgeon Sir Herbert Hamilton. Jemma Redgrave stars in the title role, with David Calder as her father, and Robert Hardy as Sir Herbert

programme.desc: William and Hester tell daughter Emma about their decision to move to France, and set about finding somewhere suitable to live. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.desc: Phil confronts Ian and threatens him, Ruth cooks a special meal for Mark. Debbie's ex boss pays her a visit and David plays dangerous games with Cindy

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Part one of two. The staff at Holby put aside their differences and prepare to separate the conjoined twins. However, the Press have latched on to the fact that a man accused of child abuse is funding the risky procedure - and Jayne is forced to explain her decisions to the board. Meanwhile, Maddy persuades Kyla to forget her past and give in to the advances of her patient admirer

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: First in a two-part story which sees emergency staff members involved in a devastating train crash. Still full of excitement following their recent engagement, Fin and Comfort escape from a compromising situation in a toilet cubicle during the derailment to find a train full of panicking commuters. Meanwhile, a passenger struggles to keep calm as the full horror of her predicament becomes clear

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gillian Taylforth joins the cast as Nikki Wright, a Sergeant from the Barton Street station who finds herself caught up in an armed stand-off along with her Sun Hill colleagues. Her quick thinking defuses a tense situation and earns the praise of visiting DAC Georgia Hobbs, who recommends her for a transfer. James Tennant's life continues to fall apart as he is arrested for drink driving and assault

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy gets suspicious about Grant's strange nocturnal behaviour. Ruth and Mark get their wires crossed over the issue of children and Ian is in trouble with Phil again

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil confronts Ian and threatens him, Ruth cooks a special meal for Mark. Debbie's ex boss pays her a visit and David plays dangerous games with Cindy

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Snowdrifts cut off Arden House on the night before Hogmanay, forcing Janet and the doctors to face a long evening huddled in front of a roaring fire. As the hours drag on, tempers wear thin and a few old scores are settled before the crowing cock heralds the dawn. Festive special of the Highland medical drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen, Jessica Turner and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: A hypochondriac becomes the bane of Joanna's life, wasting time and resources with a string of fictitious illnesses, while Andrew attempts to help a girl's parents come to terms with her deafness. Medical drama, starring Gary Mavers and Haydn Gwynne

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James holds the reputation of the practice in his hands by agreeing to judge the Darrowby Show, but has a hard time appeasing the proud local farmers. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hestor and William find the move to France quite an ordeal as she misplaces their passports and he forgets to drive on the right. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Hollywood beckons as Tom and Diana put on their directors' hats and make a promotional video, foiling Harvey's devious schemes in the process. Stephanie Cole, Graham Crowden and Daniel Hill star

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: British troops save Edith and Rene from execution, and the pair are released into the woods. Meanwhile, the Germans decide to head for Spain, and Michelle joins Crabtree in a duet. Classic wartime sitcom, starring Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy seems to have gained a stalker, but soon realises he has greater worries after discovering he promised to demonstrate archery at the vicar's garden party - while riding a horse. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Keith Clifford

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers while holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London - a case involving a cleric whose disappearance is seemingly linked to an Irish train robbery. Feature-length mystery, starring Joan Hickson, Caroline Blakiston and Joan Greenwood

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and her seasoned colleagues reopen the case of a political aide's murder when a dormant offshore bank account containing £40,000 is discovered in his name. Ben Ransley was killed in 1997 on the evening of Labour's landslide victory and in his wallet was a photo of a beautiful blonde. But neither his mother nor his best friend was able to identify the mystery woman. Is she the key to the crime? Drama, with former Brookside star John McArdle (Billy Corkhill), Jason Durr of Heartbeat fame and one-time Bad Girls actress Kika Mirylees joining Denis Lawson, Dennis Waterman and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The agents observe jailed Algerian terrorist Malik, whom they suspect of planning an attack from his cell. In a risky move, he is released so MI5 can track him to his movement's headquarters - but the team's suspicions about him are incorrect. Jimi Mistry and Joanna Roth guest star in the high-octane drama, with Rupert Penry-Jones and Peter Firth

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gillian Taylforth joins the cast as Nikki Wright, a Sergeant from the Barton Street station who finds herself caught up in an armed stand-off along with her Sun Hill colleagues. Her quick thinking defuses a tense situation and earns the praise of visiting DAC Georgia Hobbs, who recommends her for a transfer. James Tennant's life continues to fall apart as he is arrested for drink driving and assault

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: After the Second Battle of Bull Run, Hopkins and Emma set out to rescue a stranded group of wounded Union soldiers. Lisette, a hospital observer, discovers the truth about a young soldier, shocking Foster. Hopkins and Emma share an intimate moment.

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor opens her new infirmary for the poor, but also agrees to help in her father's private practice. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park and Manchester City v Arsenal at Etihad Stadium. It was 10 years to the day that Everton last recorded a victory over their neighbours, but they had started the season well. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking to avenge Arsenal's 2-0 victory when the sides last met three months ago in an FA Cup semi-final. Plus, Chelsea v Southampton, Leicester City v Aston Villa, Newcastle United v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Fulham and West Bromwich Albion v Burnley

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a terraced house in Ramsbottom in Bury, a former shop in Gravesend in Kent and a house in Newcastle-under-Lyme

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by Nick Hall and Danny Sebastian at Mona Showground on Anglesey, and pays a visit to a local jewellery maker to find out more about amber

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Cheddar Gorge in Somerset to hear the origins of the hymn Rock of Ages, while Gemma Hunt meets worship leader Martin Smith and new Christian group Bright City

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple returning from living in Sweden for 25 years who want to find a home in Wiltshire, and goes for a ride with a professional carriage driver

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Wales, upcycling expert EJ Osborne uses his salvaging expertise at Llanfoist Recycling Centre near Abergavenny by searching for three items that he can rescue and revamp. A pair of bench ends are worked on by woodworking specialists Josh and Oli, while designer maker Rupert Blanchard is given the tall order of transforming a tallboy chest of drawers. For his own project, EJ has a collection of glass jars to keep him busy

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live in the desert, including a pride of desert lions that risks hunting a giraffe several times their size. The film also features an immense swarm of locusts, sand grouse flying for 120 miles to gather water for their offspring, and an unprecedented recording of a tiny bat defending itself against one of the world's deadliest scorpions

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley visits Box Hill in Surrey to take on its zigzag cycle route - pitting himself against double Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Joanna Rowsell Shand. Plus, there are clips from the Countryfile archives showcasing the best thrills and spills in the countryside

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce shares some of the best unseen items from recent events, from a possible Banksy to an African spear linked to the explorer David Livingstone. Other finds include a Dior necklace, a collection of 1960s airline cabin bags and a remarkable skeleton clock, as well as two items related to the Suffragettes

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Cyprus on a budget rental car adventure and sample the exciting new sport of car water-skiing. Back at the test track, Chris and Paddy check out the latest family cars from Audi and Lamborghini

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Celebrating his win in a newspaper libel case, cabinet minister Peter Laurence is summoned to Downing Street to see PM Dawn Ellison, who reveals she is looking to promote him to an office of state. However, he is soon bought back down to earth with a bump when his special adviser Duncan Knock reveals an inmate in a women's prison is claiming to have a secret about his past that could affect his future. Thriller by David Hare, starring Hugh Laurie and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from Crystal Palace v Brighton & Hove Albion at Selhurst Park and Tottenham Hotspur v West Ham United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Palace and Brighton shared the spoils in a 1-1 draw here last season, and Spurs won 2-0 against their London rivals here four months ago. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur and Reading v Manchester City

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob catches up with celebrated Turner Prize-winning artist Chris Ofili, as he completes work on The Caged Bird's Song, a new piece set to receive its exhibition debut at London's National Gallery. Best known as a painter, Ofili switches mediums with his latest piece, working alongside expert weavers in Edinburgh to create a wool tapestry seven meters wide and three meters tall. With The Caged Bird's Song, Ofili aims to demonstrate his ongoing fascination with classical mythology and contemporary 'demigods', while also paying tribute to the landscape of his adopted island home of Trinidad

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunnifor hear from fraud victims who say they just could not get through to their banks on the phone quickly enough

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a flat and former shop in Haverfordwest in Pembrokeshire, a maisonette in Raynes Park, London and a run-down house in the Birmingham suburb of Erdington

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers go undercover to identify shops illegally selling knives to children, and they unmask a car parking scam where vehicles are being left in muddy fields

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Glasgow's Special Operations team is called to a car crash in dangerously wet weather, while an ambulance call respond to a report of a man in his thirties having a heart attack

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross raids the archives for the most memorable buys over the past 20 years, with early appearances from David Dickinson, Tim Wonnacott, Kate Bliss and Phil Serrell

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards returns with the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is joined by three entrepreneurs to watch customers test and score a multi-purpose beauty tool, a stoneware oven-proof breadmaker and a rear-view mirror for cyclists

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a south London couple find a new home in the High Weald in south-east England, and also visits a wildlife reserve in the Kent countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves old tents, four dining chairs and a bamboo dressing screen from being thrown in the tip, hoping she and two others can make money by restoring the items

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people to the room, with a lazy Susan, Italian jewellery, a cigar case, and a ship's wheel on offer to the five professional dealers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Colin Jackson investigates the hidden world of eating disorders in British sport, speaking to athletes from amateur to elite level and discussing his own problems with food when he was a 110m hurdler. He asks why those involved in sport are far more likely to have an eating disorder than the rest of the population, and talks to UK Sport chair Katherine Grainger about what the authorities should be doing to tackle the problem.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: It's the first time Mike has been left alone with the ghosts at night, but he assures Alison she should go out and let her hair down. The ghosts happily pass the time with music club, but they're interrupted by some uninvited guests and becomes their mission to try to alert Mike and figure out how to protect the house from the intruders.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and author David Walliams delves into his family history, learning of his paternal great-grandfather's traumatic experiences during the First World War and unearthing the story of an ancestor who became blind as the result of pioneering eye surgery in the 1880s

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Fern Brady hosts as the showcase for emerging comic talent visits Glasgow, introducing three guests. Edinburgh native Kimi Loughton discusses the joys of being a godmother, Andy Field casts his eye over everyday mundane things, and Toussaint Douglas enlightens the locals on what it's like to grow up in Lewisham

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: Kazim and Jeremiah are two young entrepreneurs in the early stages of rolling out their home delivery service, Speedi-Kazz. Both are blessed with different skill sets - Kazim has the energy and confidence, while Jeremiah has the brains. However, on occasion these differences can leave them at loggerheads. Comedy series, following on from the earlier pilot, starring Kayode Ewumi and Trieve Blackwood-Cambridge

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Janey Godley and journalist Helen Lewis joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Legendary football manager Arsene Wenger talks about his autobiography My Life in Red and White, while Hollywood star Samuel L Jackson - joined by his wife LaTanya Richardson Jackson - discusses presenting the documentary Enslaved: The Lost History of the Transatlantic Slave Trade. Actress and comedian Dawn French promotes her new novel Because of You, and cricketer and Top Gear presenter Freddie Flintoff reflects on his new book Right, Said Fred, which mocks the absurdities of modern life. Plus, recent Mercury Prize-winner Michael Kiwanuka performs his current single Light and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford investigate holiday companies offering special cleaning services in the wake of Covid-19

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Devon, Lancashire and Derby, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers crack down on rogue landlords housing families in squalid conditions, and catch a conman posing as a collector for a children's cancer charity

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: A crew are called to a festival worker who needs treatment for a reaction to a bee sting, while an elderly patient with labyrinthitis needs to be taken to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp looks back on some of the experts' best and worst buys from the past 20 years of the series

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox and try out weighted blankets to help children sleep, car cleaning products, and a microwaveable vegan mug cake made that can be baked in just 60 seconds.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton leads a couple in their search for a countryside home in the border between Staffordshire and Derbyshire border on a budget of £320,000. Later, Alistair puts his tailoring skills to the test when he visits the manufacturing factory of heritage knitwear brand John Smedley

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues a drop-arm sofa, an old chest and a train set from Witley Recycling Centre, enlisting the help of restorer Peter Fournel and upcycler Jacqui Joseph

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: An 11-year-old tries to sell his vintage scooter at auction, while a unique maths tool, an unusual birthing chair, and a 1930s shop till also go under the hammer

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Gethin Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming: Compilation
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of High Court enforcement officers, who strive to retrieve money for others. Rob and Gerald brave the red carpet at a celebrity hotspot in London to try to recover the unpaid wages of a former worker. There is an angry confrontation in a London motorcycle shop when agents try to remove goods to collect a debt owed to a customer, and Michael and Jess help a man who was forced out of his job while recovering from cancer. Can they get him the large sum of money a tribunal awarded him?

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The lives of the neighbours collide during one night as secrets come to light. Henry tries to keep Gail's decision, and its consequences, a secret from friends and family, while Hannah and Liam host a tense dinner party, and David and Saira begin a new stage in their lives. Neil makes Belle an offer that leads to a confrontation with the neighbours

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: After much discussion with the regulars in The Clansman, Jack arranges a birthday surprise for Victor's 75th - a trip down the River Kelvin in an inflatable dinghy. The duo are joined on their voyage by Winston, but the relaxing pleasure cruise soon turns into a nightmare. Comedy, starring Ford Kiernan, Greg Hemphill and Paul Riley

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: Craiglang finds itself in the grip of high summer, and charismatic codgers Jack and Victor get themselves hot under the collar fighting off competition for the prime spot in the park. Meanwhile, Isa receives news about her ex-husband Harry that changes her life for ever. Sitcom, starring Ford Kiernan and Greg Hemphill

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Cyprus on a budget rental car adventure and sample the exciting new sport of car water-skiing. Back at the test track, Chris and Paddy check out the latest family cars from Audi and Lamborghini

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: A call comes in from London Fire Brigade requesting assistance at a fire in a block of flats which immediately puts the team under pressure. Meanwhile, mother and daughter team Nicola and Maisie have just finished with their first patient of the night but are immediately dispatched to a 12-year-old boy who has had an unfortunate accident

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter K, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In an edition focusing on the harvest, Adam Henson takes viewers through the toughest one of his career on his Gloucestershire farm. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith picks grapes from sun-ripened Welsh vines and Matt Baker races to keep up with the booming strawberry harvest in Yorkshire

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don prepares his pond for the season ahead and prunes a climbing rose. Adam Frost offers ideas for a winter display of containers and the programme catches up with Frances Tophill on her allotment in Kent, to find out about her growing year. Plus, another chance to see Carol Klein's autumnal visit to Hidcote Gardens in Gloucestershire, and a couple whose garden has been inspired by the Far East

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond joins chef Nick Nairn at one the most beautiful fishing locations in Scotland, the Lake of Menteith. The duo fish for rainbow trout then cook a delicious dish by the side of the lake. Anne Lundon investigates the need to adapt the landscape as extreme weather events become more frequent amid climate change. In Dundee, Arlene Stuart meets a furniture-maker who crafts pieces from brewing waste. Plus, entomologist Gabby Flynn reveals why wasps are vital for the ecosystem

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Chris Beardshaw and wife Frances take on a new allotment and are starting from scratch to create an easy, low-maintenance fruit and vegetable patch. Kirsty Wilson is looking to extend the season by using late-flowering perennials that complement autumn colours and Brian Cunningham takes advantage of the season to plant cost-effective bare root trees. To conclude the season, the team enjoys a video call - including a surprise visitor to the party

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: In the final episode, Mary travels to the Scottish Highlands. Surrounded by magical scenery, snow-capped mountains and frost covered forests, Mary makes delicious food to warm her up after a day out in the cold, including a sausage and pepper hotpot, simple twice-baked souffles, and an onion and sage open puff pastry tart

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King conclude their culinary journey around Asia in South Korea, a country whose food is set to be the next big thing in the UK. On a night out with young people in the capital Seoul, they sample the Korean take on barbecued food and fried chicken, then head south for a local squid festival

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len Goodman collects TV and radio presenter Nicholas Parsons in a Wolseley motor, and takes him back to Studland, Dorset, where he went on holiday in 1939. Len surprises Nicholas with an authentic 1930s campervan in the spot he originally camped in 76 years ago, before heading out on their bicycles. Later, they dine of a meal of mince and spuds, enjoy a game of cricket, and play Just a Minute after a quick dip in the sea

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction

programme.title: She Wore a Yellow Ribbon (1949)
programme.desc: A cavalry officer on the brink of retirement takes drastic steps to prevent an impending war with the Arapaho people, stirred up by double-dealing agents and Custer's recent defeat. After a series of skirmishes and minor victories, he decides the best solution is to ride into the Arapaho camp and negotiate a truce. John Ford Western, starring John Wayne, Joanne Dru, John Agar and Ben Johnson

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms narrates a history of Westerns, featuring archive interviews with stars including Gary Cooper, James Stewart, Yul Brynner, Paul Newman, John Wayne and Clint Eastwood

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: The genealogy documentary follows more celebrities as they trace their family trees to uncover facts about their heritage. First up is Doctor Who star Jodie Whittaker, who looks into a romantic story she has been told about how her paternal grandmother Greta came to be given the middle name of Verdun - which was a First World War battle. On her mother's side, Jodie gets to the bottom of how her great-great-grandfather worked his way up from child labourer in a Yorkshire coal mine to becoming the owner of one

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Essex, the crew at Walton and Frinton gets an unusual mayday call to a ship called HMS Pickle. Arriving on scene, they discover it is not the modern naval vessel they expected, but a replica of a ship that once served in Nelson's fleet, and though the original survived the battle of Trafalgar, this one is shipping water and sinking fast. On the east coast of Scotland, the crew at Anstruther faces a tricky rescue trying to extract a walker with a badly broken leg from a remote coastal path. And in the Shannon basin in Ireland, the volunteers at one of the RNLI's few inland stations at Lough Derg are woken in the early hours of the morning by their pagers - cries for help have been heard coming from the 40-mile-long lake. After launching into inky darkness and thick fog, the crew make an eerie discovery

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Entrepreneurs pitch more money-making ideas to panellists Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones. Two Syrian refugees share their alternative to Cypriot halloumi, a couple showcase some virus filtration gadgets, and a charitable entrepreneur has a plan for second-hand clothes. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin recalls the making of his third travel series Full Circle, a journey around the Pacific Rim which took him to Russia, China, Vietnam, Australia and New Zealand and North and South America. This series saw Michael adopting a more investigative role than in previous odysseys, particularly when encountering the drug wars and poverty that plagued Colombia. With contributions from David Attenborough and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Enslaved with Samuel L Jackson
programme.desc: A look at those who resisted slavery, including the famous Underground Railroad and freedom boats that helped runaway slaves to escape to the safety of Canada, using a network of safe houses to make the perilous journey north. The programme also examines the influence of famous freedom fighter Harriet Tubman and the black soldiers who served in the Union Army during the Civil War

programme.title: Detroit (2017)
programme.desc: A group of rogue police officers responds to a complaint with apparent retribution rather than justice in mind, launching a raid on a group of African-Americans. Public anger reaches a peak after the incident, as relationships sour with authority. Fact-based drama set during the 1967 Detroit riots, starring John Boyega, Anthony Mackie and Algee Smith

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: The extraordinary group face moments of peril and pain in New York's Kirby Plaza as Isaac's predictions for the future begin to unfold. Drama, starring Hayden Panettiere and Masi Oka

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Four months after Peter and Sylar's showdown in New York City, Hiro remains in the past, while Claire and her family have started a new life in California. New heroes have emerged, including siblings Alejandro and Maya, who are on the run in the Dominican Republic, and Monica Dawson in New Orleans. Masi Oka and Hayden Panettiere star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The presenter enters the glitzy world of awards and prizes - for books, movies, as well as poetry, museums and gardens - examining how they influence what art is created and what culture society consumes. She also reflects on if the pandemic has helped people see beyond the glamour and red carpets and reassess what prizes are really for. Contributors include Emmy-nominated actor Brian Cox, and Bernardine Evaristo, who last year shared the Booker Prize with Margaret Atwood

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Presenter Sara Cox is joined by comedians Phil Wang, Sara Pascoe and Tom Allen and singer Sophie Ellis-Bextor. The panellists share their favourite books, revealing those that make them laugh and the fictional characters they would like to meet. They also discuss this week's book group pick, The Midnight Library by Matt Haig and Sara Pascoe's latest book, Sex, Power, Money

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Firefighters in Newcastle and Humberside are out on shift in the days leading up to Bonfire Night, the most dangerous time of the year. Officers from Merseyside Police attempt a dangerous rescue when a car gets stuck in the River Mersey with the driver trapped inside

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp visits the Mackintosh at the Willow tearooms in Glasgow to celebrate all things 20th century and collectable. Plus, expert Ben Cooper gives the lowdown on his family connection with Whitefriars glass and Tim Weeks shows off some collectable tech

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson celebrates the humble apple on his allotment and Carole Baxter revisits a hedgerow she helped plant 17 years ago to see it in all its berry bird-feeding glory. Kirsty Wilson creates a 'desertscape' with cacti in her Edinburgh flat and there's a tour of Branklyn Gardens, Perth, with head gardener Alistair Chalmers

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for chub on the river Wye in Herefordshire, Bob making an unexpected arrival by rowing boat. They are joined on today's trip by Dr Anand Patel from the Centre for Men's Health for a discussion of medical matters, and spend the night in a converted dovecote in the idyllic grounds of a 700-year-old manor house

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert art valuer Kate Bliss, trader Danny Sebastian and upcycler Stu Abbott head to Ludlow to find bargains they can turn into profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: After months of saying he is not ready to do so, George finally asks Kate out on a date to the Weld cinema, but the news quickly travels around the Weld

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett challenges the contestants to make garments from recycled materials, from curtains and soft furnishings to the offcuts left from previous episodes

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: For thousands of years, the Kazakh people of western Mongolia have formed a hunting partnership with golden eagles to help them catch furs to stay warm in the freezing winters. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan joins a family of nomads to learn how they do this, but first he must learn how to train and fly his young golden eagle. Gordon spends days with his bird to build her trust - even letting her sleep in his tent. But it's not just the eagle he has to bond with - he has to get to grips with carrying an eagle on horseback, as well as mucking in with daily chores. Eventually, he faces some of the area's top hunters in a tournament. Will he have made enough progress?

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk, Anita Manning discovers a Victorian dynamometer used for measuring the pressure of steam and David Harper is taken back to the days of ocean liners with a wine stopper from 1912. Presenter Paul Martin visits Cromer to find out about the RNLI's most decorated lifeboatman

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mike Bushell, Aisling Bea, Sunetra Sarker and Dion Dublin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Davie Milne skippers trawler Faithlie to Norwegian waters to trial a new style of fishing net, and it doesn't take long for first mate Jakey and the rest of the crew to get to grips with the new technology. In the waters off Orkney, Iain Harcus is aiming for high-value white fish on his boat Aalskere, while back in Peterhead a new scheme is under way to provide the vital skills needed to work in the fishing industry

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: JB Gill takes a stroll along the river, from the Kent Downs, through chocolate-box villages, the cathedral city of Canterbury and on towards the port of Sandwich and Pegwell Bay. Along the way, he visits a grand house that is pictured on the £10 note, views a giant crown carved in chalk on a hillside and finds a section of the river walk designed specifically for wheelchair users

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites more contestants to brave the black chair to answer question on the specialist subjects of Armistead Maupin's Tales of the City, Blackadder, the Boxer Rebellion and Blake's 7, before each is tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as school friends the Barons take on family team the Whitcombes. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Precise act, Increase tension, Fast press, and Remove tract

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions in the first of the highest-scoring loser matches, as two teams of students get a second opportunity to earn a place in the next stage of the competition

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: The live-streaming of an attack on mosques in Christchurch, New Zealand, in March 2019 heralds a new era of terrorism and confirms the far right as a fast-growing threat. Survivor Abdul Aziz describes how he was praying when a fellow worshipper was shot through a window, and he ran outside to confront the attacker, while analyst Jacob Davey recalls monitoring the digital footprint of the attack

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show from his garage. A diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life - including Romesh's mother - are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today and the latest talking points in the media. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined remotely by a selection of celebrity guests, comedians and experts, to investigate how and why the events in the news matter to people across the country

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: Four years after Donald Trump unexpectedly became president of the most powerful nation on earth, this documentary series tells the inside story of this most extraordinary presidency, with contributions from key players including Steve Bannon, Sean Spicer, Omarosa Manigault Newman and Anthony Scaramucci. The first episode charts the first 18 months, covering Trump's change of lifestyle as he moved into the White House, his fraught relationship with the press and his downgrading of White House briefings - preferring instead to talk directly to the public through Twitter

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In an edition focusing on the harvest, Adam Henson takes viewers through the toughest one of his career on his Gloucestershire farm. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith picks grapes from sun-ripened Welsh vines and Matt Baker races to keep up with the booming strawberry harvest in Yorkshire

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Thirty minutes into the night shift, police request urgent assistance at the scene of an incident - a man has called to say his mum has been stabbed. On arrival paramedics find she is in cardiac arrest, and she is declared the sickest patient in London by Ambulance Control. Across town, Josh and Vinny are dealing with more practical issues. They've been dispatched to their first patient of the night - a woman who's collapsed in a casino. But to reach her, they need to first navigate Leicester Square, which is currently teeming with revellers. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Emergency service workers aid a dog handler, who has become impaled on a spiked fence while chasing down a suspect. Plus, police officers in Kent get to grips with taser training

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves old tents, four dining chairs and a bamboo dressing screen from being thrown in the tip, hoping she and two others can make money by restoring the items

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As autumn gets underway, Monty Don gives advice on what plants need to be taken under cover now, what can stay out until after the first frosts and others that just need an extra layer of protection. Plus, he shows what can be planted now in the vegetable garden. Joe Swift visits Good Morning Britain presenter Kate Garraway in her London garden and discusses how it has been a sanctuary over the months her husband has been seriously ill. The programme also returns to Nick Bailey's home to see the results of the design project he started in his own back garden earlier in the year and meets a Kent woman who has filled her garden with exotic and tropical plants

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Quiz show in which a team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques auctioneer Charles Hanson, trader Linda Lambert and luxury upcycler Zoe Pocock head to Cambridge to search for bargains, competing to make the most money

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Weld is over-run with holiday-makers and the locals no longer have the beach to themselves - and when Woody braves the ocean, a collision with a jet-ski puts him in a coma

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The quarter-finalists face British and Irish fabrics week. First up is the pattern challenge, which is a stern test of their soft tailoring skills, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask the sewers to create a 'Workers Jacket' from linen. Next, it's the transformation challenge, and the sewers are asked to take inspiration from the British seaside by turning stripy deckchairs and parasols into stylish garments in just 90 minutes. Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan travels across Ethiopia to find out how people can live so close to hyenas, powerful carnivores that weigh up to 14 stone and are capable of killing lions in packs. He starts his journey in the capital Addis Ababa, where hyenas prowl the outskirts for food and are tolerated because people believe they ward off the devil. He also meets cattle-herders who dislike hyenas because of attacks on their livestock, and visits the medieval city of Harar, which has a reputation for having hundreds of hyenas, many of which inhabit the local graveyard

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the historic venues from the series, as well as new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin uncovers the history of the border town Berwick-upon-Tweed

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mike Bushell, Aisling Bea, Sunetra Sarker and Dion Dublin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: During a trip, there is a celebration on board Westro as apprentice Stephen West turns 20 and officially receives his skipper's licence, while fishing boat Sardonyx is on the hunt for squid in the Moray Firth. In Peterhead, part-timer Arthur James McKenzie is following in his grandfather's footsteps fishing for lobsters just 50 metres from shore

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Al Murray and Paul Chowdhry join experts James Braxton and Natasha Raskin Sharp on the hunt for bargains to sell at auction. A classic Rolls Royce and an Aston Martin take the two comedians west of London and while Al picks up some antiques with a distinct military flavour, Paul discovers he's a 'hustler' when haggling for collectables and curios. Eventually the pair head to auction in Buckinghamshire

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The Fraserburgh crew race to save a stricken fishing boat without a working rudder in a force-eight gale. On Loch Ness, a yacht becomes trapped on a weir, and one wrong move by the lifeboat crew could send it over the edge. On the north Devon coast, the Minehead crew comes to the aid of a puppy that has swallowed a fishing hook

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A team of transplant surgeons perform a double lung transplant operation, a procedure that must be carried out against the clock to avoid tissue deterioration. They successfully remove one diseased lung and replace it with a healthy one. However, just as they cut off the blood supply to the second lung, the transplanted organ stops working. At Addenbrooke's, a neurosurgeon removes a bundle of abnormal blood vessels sitting near the top of a patient's spinal cord

programme.title: Out of Her Mind
programme.desc: Sitcom, written by and starring Sara Pascoe, exploring heartbreak, family and how to survive them. When her sister announces she is getting engaged, Sara responds by trying to prove scientifically that love does not exist. Scared that the people she cares about are outgrowing her, Sara tries to befriend an anti-social fast food employee

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan. Mandy Carter is a woman with big dreams - and today her dream is to find work. But the only thing available to her is a job in a fruit packing factory, where a big mallet will come in handy. With Michelle Greenidge and Tom Basden

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Enslaved with Samuel L Jackson
programme.desc: A look at those who resisted slavery, including the famous Underground Railroad and freedom boats that helped runaway slaves to escape to the safety of Canada, using a network of safe houses to make the perilous journey north. The programme also examines the influence of famous freedom fighter Harriet Tubman and the black soldiers who served in the Union Army during the Civil War

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Checking into the Shankly this week is a party of 41 stags, who are being looked after by trainee guest relations manager James. But the morning before the lads arrive, he is nowhere to be found. Owner Lawrence challenges staff to set up a glamping festival on the roof in just one week, a loved-up couple need a last-minute bridal suite and a bride-to-be brings her friends to the hotel for her second hen party this year

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian presents a guide to medical dramas, looking back at shows including Emergency Ward 10, Dr Finlay's Casebook, Casualty, Angels and Doctors. Harry assesses each show for the authenticity of sets and costumes, celebrates some of the most frequently recurring tropes of the genre, and reveals what it takes to make a successful hospital drama

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A village becomes the scene of a violent gunfight, firearms officers fear for their lives when confronted by an armed man, and two paramedics are victims of a terrifying attack

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Mud, Sweat and Tractors: The Story of Agriculture
programme.desc: The production of fruit and vegetables in Britain. Archive material filmed by the principal of Waterperry, a college near Oxford, shows how propagation techniques revolutionised strawberry cultivation in the 1960s, while Jersey tomato growers Peter Le Maistre and Stanley Payn explain how their industry was transformed by the commercial glasshouse

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Summer returns, transforming the landscape of the famous national park from a frozen wilderness into the ideal environment for newborn animals. As temperatures rise, grizzly bears teach their young the secrets of survival - but even at this time of year, snow can sweep down from the mountains without warning

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The explorer finds that China is a world of contrasts and contradictions as he treks toward the Vietnam border via Shanghai and the Yangtze river. From the Bavarian-style hotel at Qingdao, once occupied by Chairman Mao, he tackles the 6,293 steps of China's holiest mountain, sees the world's largest engineering project and visits the ancient Miao tribe in the mountains of the south-west

programme.title: Collateral
programme.desc: A pizza delivery man is shot dead in south London, seemingly in a random act of violence, but DI Kip Glaspie is unconvinced and discovers that the takeaway manager inexplicably sent a Syrian refugee instead of his regular driver. Kip and her partner DS Nathan Bilk track down the victim's home, where they find his sisters, who the detective is convinced are hiding something. Crime thriller by David Hare, starring Carey Mulligan, John Simm, Nicola Walker and Billie Piper

programme.title: Collateral
programme.desc: Pizza shop manager Laurie has gone missing and a fatigued Kip discovers a hidden stash of cash while searching his house for clues, while Nathan learns Mikey is using Regal Pizza as a front to deliver drugs. David reveals to the police that Linh thought the killer was a woman, and Kip and Nathan interview Fatima at Harsfleet, with MI5 operative Sam Spence in attendance, where she claims Abdullah was killed because he had an explosive piece of information. Crime thriller, starring Carey Mulligan and John Simm

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Dawn's world unravels after Jake's disappearance. Harmony in the Slater household proves short-lived when Jean and Roxy discover Sean's secret

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Roxy and Sean's bombshell throws the Slater household into chaos. Shirley resolves to teach Phil a lesson

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Roxy's big day arrives, but there is no sign of Sean. Shirley risks Phil's wrath in order to help Ben achieve his dreams

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ronnie makes it to the church, and Shirley takes on Phil following Ben's appearance in the talent show

programme.title: Mud, Sweat and Tractors: The Story of Agriculture
programme.desc: The production of fruit and vegetables in Britain. programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Summer returns, transforming the landscape of the famous national park from a frozen wilderness into the ideal environment for newborn animals. As temperatures rise, grizzly bears teach their young the secrets of survival - but even at this time of year, snow can sweep down from the mountains without warning

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: The former steeplejack sets out on an enthusiastic tour of Britain's industrial heritage, exploring the early use of wind and water power and visiting some steam engines still in use today. On his travels the presenter visits old Lancashire to observe the workings of the Bolton, Bury and Manchester Canal

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a peaceful scene featuring the undaunted splendour of the sky, trees and a lake

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould investigate a portrait of a man wearing a black cravat painted in 1939, which the owner believes is an early work of Lucian Freud, from his time at the Central School of Art - even though Freud himself denied painting it. The team tries to uncover who the mysterious man in the painting was, whether it really was painted by Freud, and if so, why did he claim that it was not his work. The investigation digs up a complex history of feuds, rivalry and intrigue

programme.title: The Billion Dollar Art Hunt
programme.desc: John Wilson follows a tip-off that the world's biggest art theft from Boston's Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum in 1990, valued at a billion dollars, may be about to be found - not in America, but in Dublin

programme.title: Collateral
programme.desc: Faced with a second murder, Kip and Nathan struggle to figure out how the two killings are connected, with the senior detective convinced that MI5 is leaking information to the media. Local MP David Mars courts controversy as he rails against the press, and he also takes his ex-wife Karen to task, confronting her about her addictions, while the Rev Jane Oliver gets a visit from the bishop, who warns that he can't turn a blind eye to her love life any more. Carey Mulligan, John Simm and Nicola Walker star

programme.title: Collateral
programme.desc: The police use the video footage Fatima provided to arrest Mehmet and Bhuran, while Nathan tips off Sam Spence about the arrests. In Westminster, Mars finally faces party leader Deborah Clifford and is given a final chance to toe the line, but his main focus is on his private life. Back at the station, Jack Haley harangues Kip for offering Fatima residency, something she is unable to guarantee. Conclusion of David Hare's crime thriller, starring Carey Mulligan, John Simm, Nicola Walker and Billie Piper

programme.title: Inside Cinema
programme.desc: Documentary from Grace Barber-Plentie on trailblazing film director Barry Jenkins, whose Best Picture winner Moonlight made Oscar night history in 2017 - beating La La Land to Best Picture following a dramatic and unprecedented envelope mix-up

programme.title: Inside Cinema
programme.desc: Documentary from Kambole Campbell exploring the work of Ryan Coogler, director of Fruitvale Station, Creed and the box-office sensation Black Panther

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: An exploration of the textile mills and factories that once thrived in Britain during the Industrial Revolution. From New Lanark in Scotland to the mill town of Burnley, Fred meets ex-workers to discover what life was really like, and chats to a group that has dedicated 30 years to restoring mill engines

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Mountains seem to sing in harmony with the sky as Bob Ross' brushes tell a story in breathtaking hues and depth

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the history of the Norse warriors, delving into their customs and searching for the truth behind their fearsome reputation. He begins by telling the story of their origins, and investigates the world of their ancient ancestors. The programme charts Scandinavia's prehistoric landscape, from the Baltic Sea to Norway's Atlantic coast, and Neil examines archaeological evidence to create a portrait of the society from which the Vikings emerged

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: Neil Oliver presents the drama-documentary telling the tale of the bloody 17th century Civil War. When the Scottish Parliament, led by the Chief of Clan Campbell, declares war on Charles I, Clans loyal to the King rise up in rebellion. At the heart of this epic struggle lies an ancient feud between the Campbells and the MacDonalds, and the Royalist Clans use the war to wreak bloody vengeance on their rivals in an epic conflict that threatens the stability of the newly emerging British state

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: One-off drama by Jim Hawkins and Horace Ove, based on the events surrounding the Spaghetti House siege - an armed robbery that went disastrously wrong. Starring T-Bone Wilson, Trevor Thomas and Archie Pool. Originally broadcast in 1979

programme.title: I Am Not Your Negro (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary-maker Raoul Peck uses an unfinished manuscript by James Baldwin, an account of the lives and successive assassinations of civil rights leaders Medgar Evans, Malcolm X and Martin Luther King Jr, as the springboard for this examination of the black experience in America. Narrated by Samuel L Jackson, the film uses Baldwin's words as it explores everything from Hollywood stereotypes to police brutality

programme.desc: Documentary following Chinese fashion designer Guo Pei as she aims to switch from dressing her country's elite to trying to conquer the world of haute couture in Paris

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: A guided tour by 16-year-old Syrian boy, Ahmed, through one of the biggest refugee camps in the world - Zaatari in Jordan. Ahmed is like any other boy his age, except he's had to leave everything behind, including his toys and technology, to move to the refugee camp. Life in Zaatari is nothing compared to home. When the Syrian civil war began in 2012 his family had to abandon their affluent lives and flee across the border to Zaatari camp with thousands of others for safety. Now living in a storage container for a home after his house was destroyed in the civil war, he has lived in Zaatari for three years, along with 80,000 other refugees, who can't leave the camp without permission. Despite their camp having everything they need, from shops to a hospital, school and football pitches, life is very different to how it was before. Ahmed doesn't let this affect his positivity though, and he finds new ways to have fun with his friends - even if it is in a desert sandstorm followed by flood

programme.title: Twirlywoos

programme.title: The Baby Club

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Toybox

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid becomes a hairdresser and arrives at Madame Frou-Frou's salon at the start of a hectic day - and the team is expecting a very important customer

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on an early morning walk to spot some cloud shapes. On the way, they see the city waking up, find a funny mirror, spot shapes in the clock tower and even catch a big fish!

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: After having a bath, Pablo is upset that his smell is gone. He enlists the help of Bubble Bottle and his friends to track down his smell and follow it down the drain

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore an eskimo disco and a magical jellyfish wallpaper, and watch as Book Boy makes a rollercoaster.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Sister and brother, Isla and Noah fancy a spot of gardening and along with Mum and Dad they visit a garden centre to buy some new flowers to plant

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Angel invites Tig to a sleepover. At night the visiting youngster finds herself all alone with everyone else asleep, calling upon Woolly to help her survive the night

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: The Olobobs are excited to try Crunch's new swing, but Bobble accidentally breaks it

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Children from all over the world demonstrate their favourite recipes, and find out where the ingredients they have used come from

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie's Mum, Jess has just married Doug's Dad, Simon. The new family get home to discover a lost pup hiding in the wedding presents. Evie wants to keep him and call him Waffle

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb accidentally knocks down Otus's sandcastle with a football, and realises he has to show he is sorry as well as saying it

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: It is Halloween and the feisty girl dresses up as a pirate and to go trick-or-treating, but it is a stormy night and when she begins to feel unwell she has to take refuge at Grannie Island's house

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: After visiting a bird park, Melody is introduced to The Firebird Suite by Igor Stravinsky, and her imagination takes her into a story about a firebird being held captive by a mean ogre

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Introducing the letter M as the ever-munching M and friends make a gingerbread man and chase after him on a flying mat - using a handy map to keep them hot on his trail

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny finds a frog in the garden and wants to keep her, so he makes her a new home

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy monster has accidentally dropped his super-stacked jam sandwich snack, but wants to know why it fell down and not up

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes to the theatre with his friends to watch a sensory performance. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble puts on a magic show for Aunt Polly and Lord Tumble

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The voice trumpet leads a game of I Spy, where the Teletubbies have to spot different colours - purple, green, yellow and red

programme.title: Tinpo
